2026-06-03T10:24:08Z GPU1 A100 CUDA state-passing RWKV7 smoke at 3323172. Purpose: verify official RWKV-CUDA statepassing clampw compiles and runs forward/backward after wind movmatrix failure on sm80. Result: 1-step 4x4 smoke completed; score is not a model-quality signal.

2026-06-03T10:11:48Z GPU1 A100 wind CUDA compile probe at 65e20a5. Insight: modded-nanogpt wind source reaches ptxas but fails on movmatrix for sm80/CUDA12.6, so wind is not a viable GPU1 default. Decision: switch to official RWKV-CUDA statepassing clampw.

2026-06-03T10:30:19Z GPU1 9x9 CUDA throughput probe at 2c21f96. Insight: after switching from toy PyTorch scan to RWKV7 statepassing CUDA, step100 appears quickly and CE drops below 1.0 by step100 (0.9449), so the previous 9x9 kill was implementation throughput, not evidence against the FutureSeed hypothesis. Decision: eligible for a real 800-step 9x9 cliff run; 120-step exact=0 is not a quality conclusion.

2026-06-03T10:33:52Z GPU1 9x9 viability cliff with official RWKV7 statepassing CUDA at 92ee7b9. Hypothesis: after replacing toy PyTorch RWKV with CUDA, FutureSeed/RWKV should scale from saturated 6x6 to real 9x9 constraints. Result: holes8 loop5 exact=1.0000, holes12=0.9629, holes16=0.8535; full-board loop1=0.9629 and loop5=1.0000; K8 oracle gap ~0. Decision: continue CUDA 9x9 direction; selector work is low priority.

2026-06-03T11:16:06Z GPU1 9x9 ablation at 5e2253f. Hypothesis: remove outer recurrent loop to test whether 9x9 success is mostly one-pass local mapping. Result: holes12=0.9551, holes16=0.7715, full loop1=0.9922; compared with baseline holes16 loop5=0.8535. Decision: loop is not required for easy 8/12-hole solve, but contributes hard-hole global refinement (+0.082 holes16 vs no-loop; baseline loop gain +0.336).

2026-06-03T11:17:47Z GPU1 9x9 ablation at 5e2253f. Hypothesis: FUTURE_SEED_SCALE=0 tests whether cross-layer terminal-state seeding is structural or cosmetic. Result: train CE stayed high at step800=0.4498; full loop5 exact=0.0664, holes12=0.0195, holes16=0.0000; K8 oracle gap=0.127. Decision: FutureSeed is essential for 9x9 scaling and trajectory stability; do not spend next budget on longer no-seed training except as a targeted optimization study.

2026-06-03T11:24:49Z GPU1 9x9 ablation at 5e2253f. Hypothesis: NOISE_SCALE=0 tests whether feature-diff noise is useful regularization or harmful variance after CUDA scale-up. Result: train CE step800=0.0007, holes12=0.9766, holes16=0.9004, K8 selector gap=0.000; this beats baseline holes16=0.8535. Decision: disable or reduce training feature noise for the next 9x9 scale run; keep rollout selector work low priority when FutureSeed is enabled.

2026-06-04T04:13:35Z GPU1 EqR no-Hydra 9x9 FutureSeed+feature-diff probe at cbbfbe9. Hypothesis: EqR attractor recursion should exploit learned cross-level FutureSeed plus low-scale manifold-shaped feature noise without needing Hydra or flash_attn; if loop depth matters on holes12/16, this is a real mechanism branch rather than easy-distribution saturation. Result: train CE step600=0.0005 in 82.5s; holes8 loop4 exact=1.0000, holes12=0.9844, holes16=0.8945; loop gains are large (holes8 +0.9375, holes12 +0.9766, holes16 +0.8945). Decision: continue EqR FutureSeed direction; next high-ROI test is not broad ablation but selector/noise scheduling or harder-hole transfer. Lesson: flash_attn is unnecessary for this experiment; PyTorch CUDA SDPA is enough for seq81.

2026-06-09 h72 case-bank reproduction of the 12x12 clean frontier: h48/h60/h72/h84 loop5 exact matches prior run at 0.8880/0.5052/0.0443/0.0. Case-bank h72 eval_n=512 exported 4 solved-by-loop, 4 almost-solved, and 4 hard-failure boards. Insight: loop repairs large loop1 errors into coherent solves, but last 1-5 coupled wrong cells can become sticky.

2026-06-10 h72 diagnostics rerun on GPU1 after adding per-cell margin/entropy/stability fields: h48/h60/h72/h84 loop5 exact=0.9531/0.6484/0.0677/0.0; case-bank h72 eval_n=1024 exact=0.0742 with 8 solved-by-loop, 8 almost-solved, 8 hard-failure cases. Insight: final h72 errors are mostly stable attractors, not late-loop oscillations; 47/48 final wrong cells did not change in the last shown loop, and hard failures have higher confidence margins than almost-solved cases. Next work should change generic loop dynamics/credit assignment, not add Sudoku-specific conflict repair.

2026-06-10 loop-credit test on GPU1: delayed loop supervision from loop3 plus loop7 did not break h72 stable attractors. h48/h60/h72/h84 loop7 exact=0.8464/0.4245/0.0156/0.0, worse than the loop5 final-loss baseline 0.9531/0.6484/0.0677/0.0. Extra loops gave only +0.0078 h60 exact over loop5, while h72 case-bank exact fell to 0.0283. Final wrong cells were still stable, 48/48 unchanged in the last shown loop. Decision: stop this direction; the next lever should be FutureSeed/update damping, not more loop credit.

2026-06-10 fixed FutureSeed damping test on GPU1: FUTURE_SEED_DECAY=0.5 slowed optimization and did not improve the h72 cliff. h48/h60/h72/h84 loop5 exact=0.9062/0.5052/0.0521/0.0 versus baseline 0.9531/0.6484/0.0677/0.0; h72 case-bank exact=0.0635. Final wrong cells remained stable, only 2/48 changed in the last shown loop, and mean margin rose to 0.501. Decision: fixed damping is too blunt; only a learned update gate is still worth one direct test.

2026-06-10 learned FutureSeed update-gate test on GPU1: FUTURE_SEED_UPDATE=learned with decay init 0.5 strongly hurt optimization and transfer. h48/h60/h72/h84 loop5 exact=0.7005/0.1927/0.0/0.0; h72 case-bank exact=0.0020 and only 2 solved-by-loop cases were found. The learned update gate stayed near 0.547 and did not rescue fixed damping. Decision: stop damping/gating in this form; stable h72 attractors are not solved by simple FutureSeed inertia.

2026-06-10 hard-stage scaling on GPU1: extending the same clean FutureSeed+loop 12x12 run to 700 steps on the 60-72 stage strongly moved the h72 cliff without new Sudoku-specific tricks. h48/h60/h72/h84 loop5 exact=0.9635/0.7786/0.1745/0.0; h72 case-bank exact=0.1865, blank_acc=0.9293. Against the diagnostics baseline h72 improved 0.0677->0.1745 while damping/loop-credit variants were negative. Decision: continue clean compute/curriculum scaling before adding new mechanisms.

2026-06-10 longer hard-stage scaling on GPU1: same FutureSeed+loop 12x12 model with 60-72 stage extended to 1500 steps. h48/h60/h72/h84 loop5 exact=0.9818/0.8750/0.4010/0.0078; h72 case-bank exact=0.3955, blank_acc=0.9534. This is a strong positive over s1500 h72=0.1745, while h84 only barely opens. Decision: continue clean scaling, but shift the next question to whether targeted h84 curriculum can move the frontier.

2026-06-10 h84 frontier-shift probe on GPU1: same FutureSeed+loop 12x12 model with curriculum moved to 72-84 holes under remaining time. h60/h72/h84/h96 loop5 exact=0.9297/0.5469/0.0234/0.0; h84 case-bank exact=0.0352, blank_acc=0.8582. Targeted h84 exposure improves over h84=0.0078 from the h72-only s2300 run, but the boundary remains hard. Decision: do not repeat short h84 probes; next high-ROI path is longer h84 stage after GPU restart or capacity/loop-dynamics scaling with h84 curriculum.

2026-06-11 GPU1 h84 long hard-stage scaling: same clean FutureSeed+loop 12x12 model with 72-84 stage extended to 1500 steps. h60/h72/h84/h96 loop5 exact=0.9297/0.7161/0.1172/0.0000; h84 case-bank exact=0.1211 blank_acc=0.8884. 2026-06-11 GPU1 D320 capacity feasibility probe under short remaining lease: D320/L12/loop5 batch48 fit in memory (~49GB observed) but the 900-step shortened h84 curriculum did not reach a useful regime. Train CE step500/700/900=1.7790/1.1760/0.8789; h72/h84/h96 loop5 exact=0.0/0.0/0.0; h84 blank_acc=0.5523. Decision: do not treat width alone as validated by short probes. A real capacity test needs the same 2400-step h84 curriculum or a schedule that reaches low CE earlier; otherwise prefer loop/FutureSeed-state mechanism work.

2026-06-11 GPU1 h96 frontier-shift run: same clean FutureSeed+loop D256/L12/loop5 model, curriculum moved to 84-96 with stages 16-36:200,36-60:300,60-72:400,72-84:1000,84-96:1500. h72/h84/h96/h108 loop5 exact=0.8750/0.4740/0.0104/0.0000; h96 case-bank exact=0.0068 blank_acc=0.7652. h96 loop1=0.0000, loop3=0.0000, loop4=0.0078, loop5=0.0104. K1 oracle gap is zero. Decision: h96 is barely opened by clean curriculum/compute, but not supported; next work should be longer h96 hard-stage or simple loop/FutureSeed-state dynamics, not selector work or short width probes.

2026-06-11 GPU1 loop-time conditioning h96 mechanism run: added generic LOOP_TIME_SCALE=1 sinusoidal loop-phase conditioning to the clean D256/L12/loop5 12x12 h96 curriculum. Result is negative for final frontier despite active use of the signal. h72/h84/h96/h108 loop5 exact=0.8542/0.4115/0.0052/0.0000 versus clean h96 baseline 0.8750/0.4740/0.0104/0.0000. h96 loop1 blank_acc improves 0.4211->0.5102, but loop5 blank_acc drops 0.7769->0.7549 and h96 exact halves. Train CE ends slightly lower, 0.3667 vs 0.3931, so this is not a simple optimization failure. Lesson: a loop clock makes early predictions stronger but does not fix late global consistency. Decision: stop loop-time/feedback scale sweeps; next useful mechanism must change late-loop state revision or spend more clean h96 hard-stage compute.

2026-06-11 GPU1 h96-focused compressed curriculum probe: same clean FutureSeed+loop D256/L12/loop5, but 2400 total steps and compressed early stages 16-36:100,36-60:150,60-72:250,72-84:500,84-96:1400 due remaining lease. Purpose: test whether h96 opens by spending proportionally more time in 84-96, or whether full middle-stage foundation is required. Result: h72/h84/h96/h108 loop5 exact=0.9010/0.3828/0.0000/0.0000; h96 blank_acc=0.7545; final CE=0.3850. Against full h96 baseline 0.8750/0.4740/0.0104/0.0000 and h96 blank_acc=0.7769, compressed schedule improves h72 but loses h84/h96. Lesson: h96 is not unlocked by entering hard stage early; middle curriculum/global-consistency foundation matters. Decision: stop compressed h96 schedules; next useful path is full-length clean hard-stage compute or a real late-state revision mechanism.

2026-06-11 GPU1 full-budget clean h96 scale test: D256/L12/loop5 12x12 FutureSeed+loop at source 4337212, preserving middle curriculum and extending 84-96 to 2400 steps (16-36:200,36-60:300,60-72:500,72-84:1400,84-96:2400), batch80/eval512/bfloat16. Result: h72/h84/h96/h108 loop5 exact=0.9844/0.8828/0.3164/0.0000; h96 blank_acc=0.9264; final CE=0.1178 after 5130.8s. This strongly improves over clean h96 baseline 0.8750/0.4740/0.0104/0.0000 and over compressed h96 focus 0.9010/0.3828/0.0000/0.0000. Insight: h96 was still compute/curriculum limited, not a hard mechanism ceiling; the compressed run failed because it removed middle-stage foundation. Loop remains essential: h96 loop1/3/4/5 exact=0.0000/0.0293/0.2715/0.3164, so recurrent refinement is the solve path. K1 oracle gap is zero, so selector work remains low ROI. Decision: treat clean 12x12 h96 as supported; move frontier to h108 or simple late-loop state revision. No tag because score <0.50.

2026-06-11 GPU1 h108 clean frontier test: D256/L12/loop5 12x12 FutureSeed+loop at source e446f4a, using full h96 foundation and 96-108 hard stage (16-36:200,36-60:300,60-72:500,72-84:1400,84-96:2200,96-108:1600), batch80/eval512/bfloat16. Result: h84/h96/h108/h120 loop5 exact=0.9902/0.8848/0.1797/0.0000; h108 blank_acc=0.8685; final CE=0.1359 after 6591.7s. Insight: clean FutureSeed+loop scales beyond h96; h108 is open without Sudoku-specific repair or selector search. Loop remains the mechanism: h108 loop1/3/4/5 exact=0.0000/0.0020/0.1328/0.1797. K1 oracle gap is zero, so selector work remains low ROI. h120 is still closed with blank_acc=0.3439, making h120 the new clean 12x12 frontier. No tag because score <0.50.

2026-06-11 GPU1 h120 loop6 all-loop scale test: clean 12x12 FutureSeed+loop source 8ccc4a9, D256/L12/loop6, LOOP_LOSS=all, h120 curriculum 16-36:200,36-60:300,60-72:500,72-84:1200,84-96:1800,96-108:1800,108-120:1600, batch72/eval512/bfloat16 on GPU1 A100 80GB after AIStation restart. Result: h96/h108/h120/h132 loop6 exact=0.9922/0.9160/0.0273/0.0000; h120 blank_acc=0.5866; final CE=0.3605 after 8556.3s. Loop curve at h120: loop1/3/4/5/6 exact=0.0000/0.0117/0.0273/0.0273/0.0273; loop6 adds blank_acc but exact saturates after loop4. Insight: loop6+all-loop strongly consolidates h96/h108 and opens h120 from zero, but h120 remains a real frontier because final local accuracy is only 0.5866 and loop gains saturate early. The limiting factor is hard-stage learning/capacity/state dynamics, not selector search. K1 oracle gap is zero. Decision: treat h120 as barely opened, h108 as strongly supported by this route; next high-ROI work should target h120 stage compute/capacity or a simple FutureSeed state update, not rollout selector or Sudoku repair. No tag because primary score <0.50.

h120 hard-stage curve run at 2e95bd8. Insight: one long clean scale run with fixed checkpoint evals answers whether extra h108-120 hard-stage steps open h120. Results: h120 loop6 exact at 1k/2k/3k/final hard-stage steps = 0.0137/0.0332/0.0410/0.0449; h108 final = 0.9355; h96 final = 0.9902; h132 remains 0. This shows longer hard-stage helps but does not remove the h120 global-coupling cliff.
